summary: Backend-only verification completed for ShiftRule implementation and unit tests. Core functionality passes; one test-runner configuration issue found.

minor.issue: Running `pytest /app/tests/test_shift_rule.py` from /app fails collection with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'aceb'` unless PYTHONPATH=/app is set.

action_items: Add package path configuration so pytest works without manual PYTHONPATH (e.g., pyproject.toml pytest pythonpath config, editable install, or package init/setup alignment).
critical_code_review_comments: ShiftRule logic is correct for positive shift, wrap-around, invalid symbol error handling, and negative shift support.
Validation for empty/duplicate alphabet is present and appropriate.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Core tests pass when PYTHONPATH=/app is set. Main gap is packaging/import-path configuration for plain pytest invocation.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: run `pytest /app/tests/test_shift_rule.py -v` from /app. Result: ModuleNotFoundError for `aceb`. Root cause: project package path is not automatically on sys.path for pytest in current environment. Mitigation: configure test environment/package installation so `aceb` resolves without manual PYTHONPATH.
